title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: classify_texture() assigns USDA texture classes (sand, loam, clay, etc.) soil samples based sand, silt, clay fractions. S3 generic three dispatch methods: methods accept return tibbles (spatial equivalent), support tidy evaluation — can pass bare column names string names interchangeably.

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: Two columns appended input tibble: .texture_class — full USDA class name .texture_abbr — standard abbreviation (e.g. SaLo, Cl)
code: soils <- tribble(   ~sample_id, ~sand, ~silt, ~clay,   "A1",          65,    20,    15,   "A2",          20,    50,    30,   "A3",           8,    72,    20,   "A4",          40,    38,    22,   "A5",          52,     6,    42,   "A6",           6,    47,    47 )  classify_texture(soils, sand, silt, clay) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   sample_id  sand  silt  clay .texture_class .texture_abbr #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr>         #> 1 A1           65    20    15 sandy loam     SaLo          #> 2 A2           20    50    30 clay loam      ClLo          #> 3 A3            8    72    20 silty loam     SiLo          #> 4 A4           40    38    22 loam           Lo            #> 5 A5           52     6    42 sandy clay     SaCl          #> 6 A6            6    47    47 silty clay     SiCl

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: Column names can passed bare symbols string-named columns. function uses rlang::enquo() internally integrates naturally dplyr pipelines. Scalar values also work argument — useful constant fractions across dataset:
code: # Works inside a pipe with renamed columns soils |>   rename(s = sand, si = silt, c = clay) |>   classify_texture(sand = s, silt = si, clay = c) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   sample_id     s    si     c .texture_class .texture_abbr #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr>         #> 1 A1           65    20    15 sandy loam     SaLo          #> 2 A2           20    50    30 clay loam      ClLo          #> 3 A3            8    72    20 silty loam     SiLo          #> 4 A4           40    38    22 loam           Lo            #> 5 A5           52     6    42 sandy clay     SaCl          #> 6 A6            6    47    47 silty clay     SiCl # All samples have the same silt (25%); sand and clay vary tibble(sand = c(60, 40, 15), clay = c(15, 35, 60)) |>   classify_texture(sand = sand, silt = 25, clay = clay) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>    sand  clay .texture_class .texture_abbr #>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr>         #> 1    60    15 sandy loam     SaLo          #> 2    40    35 clay loam      ClLo          #> 3    15    60 clay           Cl

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: classify_texture() validates three fractions sum 100 (within tolerance ± 0.5). Rows don’t sum correctly raise error.
code: # Bad row: fractions sum to 95 tibble(sand = 50, silt = 30, clay = 15) |>   classify_texture(sand, silt, clay) #> Error in `check_texture_sums()`: #> ! Sand, silt, and clay must sum to 100 for every row. #> ✖ Found 1 row(s) where the sum differs from 100 by more than 1.

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: usda_texture_classes contains polygon boundary data used internally. helper lookup table 12 classes representative centroids: 12 centroids classify correctly — .texture_class column matches Class column.
code: usda_texture_classes |>   distinct(class, abbr) |>   arrange(class) #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    class           abbr   #>    <chr>           <chr>  #>  1 clay            Cl     #>  2 clay loam       ClLo   #>  3 loam            Lo     #>  4 loamy sand      LoSa   #>  5 sand            Sa     #>  6 sandy clay      SaCl   #>  7 sandy clay loam SaClLo #>  8 sandy loam      SaLo   #>  9 silt            Si     #> 10 silty clay      SiCl   #> 11 silty clay loam SiClLo #> 12 silty loam      SiLo tribble(   ~Class,            ~Abbr,     ~Sand, ~Silt, ~Clay,   "sand",            "Sa",         92,     5,     3,   "loamy sand",      "LoSa",       80,    12,     8,   "sandy loam",      "SaLo",       65,    22,    13,   "loam",            "Lo",         41,    39,    20,   "silty loam",      "SiLo",       20,    65,    15,   "silt",            "Si",          7,    85,     8,   "sandy clay loam", "SaClLo",     60,    13,    27,   "clay loam",       "ClLo",       32,    34,    34,   "silty clay loam", "SiClLo",     10,    56,    34,   "sandy clay",      "SaCl",       52,     6,    42,   "silty clay",      "SiCl",        6,    47,    47,   "clay",            "Cl",         20,    20,    60 ) |>   classify_texture(Sand, Silt, Clay) |>   select(Class, Abbr, .texture_class, .texture_abbr) #> # A tibble: 12 × 4 #>    Class           Abbr   .texture_class  .texture_abbr #>    <chr>           <chr>  <chr>           <chr>         #>  1 sand            Sa     sand            Sa            #>  2 loamy sand      LoSa   loamy sand      LoSa          #>  3 sandy loam      SaLo   sandy loam      SaLo          #>  4 loam            Lo     loam            Lo            #>  5 silty loam      SiLo   silty loam      SiLo          #>  6 silt            Si     silt            Si            #>  7 sandy clay loam SaClLo sandy clay loam SaClLo        #>  8 clay loam       ClLo   clay loam       ClLo          #>  9 silty clay loam SiClLo silty clay loam SiClLo        #> 10 sandy clay      SaCl   sandy clay      SaCl          #> 11 silty clay      SiCl   silty clay      SiCl          #> 12 clay            Cl     clay            Cl

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: vectorised backend processes N points per class simultaneously, row row. makes practical large datasets.
code: set.seed(5519) big <- tibble(   sand = runif(10000, 5, 85),   clay = runif(10000, 5, 45) ) |>   mutate(silt = 100 - sand - clay) |>   filter(silt >= 2) |>   slice_head(n = 10000)  elapsed <- system.time(   result <- classify_texture(big, sand, silt, clay) )["elapsed"]  cat(sprintf("10 000 samples in %.3f s\n", elapsed)) #> 10 000 samples in 0.026 s cat(sprintf("NAs: %d\n", sum(is.na(result$.texture_class)))) #> NAs: 0

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: data sf point object, classify_texture() detects class dispatches .sf method. Geometry preserved.
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  pts_sf <- st_as_sf(   soils,   coords = c("sand", "silt"),   # using sand/silt as dummy coordinates   crs    = NA_crs_ ) pts_sf$sand <- soils$sand pts_sf$silt <- soils$silt pts_sf$clay <- soils$clay  out_sf <- classify_texture(pts_sf, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay) class(out_sf)         # still an sf object #> [1] "sf"         "tbl_df"     "tbl"        "data.frame" names(out_sf)         # .texture_class and .texture_abbr appended #> [1] "sample_id"      "clay"           "geometry"       "sand"           #> [5] "silt"           ".texture_class" ".texture_abbr"

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: raster stacks (e.g. SoilGrids), classify_texture() dispatches .SpatRaster method returns categorical raster 12 levels. SoilGrids note: SoilGrids stores fractions g/kg (0–1000). Divide 10 classifying. classify_texture() emit cli_warn() value exceeds 100, error surfaces clearly.
code: library(terra) #> terra 1.9.11  # Build a small 10×10 test raster r <- rast(ncols = 10, nrows = 10, nlyrs = 3,           xmin = 0, xmax = 1, ymin = 0, ymax = 1) names(r) <- c("sand", "silt", "clay")  set.seed(7712) sand_v <- runif(100, 10, 70) clay_v <- runif(100, 5, 40) silt_v <- 100 - sand_v - clay_v values(r) <- cbind(sand_v, silt_v, clay_v)  r_class <- classify_texture(r, sand = "sand", silt = "silt", clay = "clay") print(r_class) #> class       : SpatRaster  #> size        : 10, 10, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.1, 0.1  (x, y) #> extent      : 0, 1, 0, 1  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (CRS84) (OGC:CRS84)  #> source(s)   : memory #> categories  : texture_class  #> name        : texture_class  #> min value   :    sandy clay  #> max value   :    sandy loam levels(r_class)[[1]] #>    id   texture_class #> 1   1            clay #> 2   2      silty clay #> 3   3      sandy clay #> 4   4       clay loam #> 5   5 silty clay loam #> 6   6 sandy clay loam #> 7   7            loam #> 8   8      silty loam #> 9   9      sandy loam #> 10 10            silt #> 11 11      loamy sand #> 12 12            sand r_sg <- rast("SoilGrids_sand_silt_clay.tif")   # g/kg layers r_class <- classify_texture(r_sg / 10,                              sand = "sand", silt = "silt", clay = "clay")

what: 8. Edge cases: boundary points and vertices
title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: ray-casting algorithm includes collinearity check points fall exactly class boundaries (shared edges, polygon vertices) classified correctly rather returning NA.
code: # Clay/silty clay boundary at clay=40%, silt=60% boundary_pts <- tribble(   ~description,          ~sand, ~silt, ~clay,   "clay vertex",             0,    40,    60,   "silt/silty clay border",  0,    60,    40,   "clay loam centroid",     32,    34,    34 )  classify_texture(boundary_pts, sand, silt, clay) |>   select(description, .texture_class) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   description            .texture_class #>   <chr>                  <chr>          #> 1 clay vertex            clay           #> 2 silt/silty clay border silty clay     #> 3 clay loam centroid     clay loam

title: Classifying soil texture with classify_texture()
text: vignette("gg-texture-triangle") — visualise classified samples texture triangle vignette("gis-workflow") — full GIS workflow sf terra

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: examples use two datasets:
code: # A handful of labelled soil samples pts <- tibble(   sand  = c(70, 20, 40, 10, 55, 30),   silt  = c(15, 30, 40, 20, 30, 45),   clay  = c(15, 50, 20, 70, 15, 25),   label = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F") )  # A denser grid of points with a synthetic soil property set.seed(4291) surf_df <- tibble(   sand = runif(90, 5, 88),   clay = runif(90, 5, 55) ) |>   dplyr::mutate(silt = 100 - sand - clay) |>   dplyr::filter(silt >= 2) |>   dplyr::mutate(prop = clay * 0.55 + sand * 0.08 + rnorm(dplyr::n(), 0, 4))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: Minimum required arguments data frame three bare column names. Sample points drawn black dots default.  Map column point colour colour:
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, colour = label) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = NULL)

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: style = "none" (default) — unfilled regions, borders .  style = "wiki" — colour scheme matching Wikipedia USDA texture diagram.
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "none") gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "wiki")

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: Control grid lines tick marks drawn. Pass numeric vector percentages 1 99. Quarters — c(25, 50, 75)  Every 10 % — seq(10, 90, 10)  Every 5 % — seq(5, 95, 5). Tick label size auto-scales .
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, breaks = c(25, 50, 75)) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, breaks = seq(10, 90, 10)) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, breaks = seq(5, 95, 5))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: three parameters control internal dashed grid lines. Darker, thicker, solid:  Dotted, light:  Hidden — set grid_linetype = "blank":
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   grid_colour    = "grey30",   grid_linetype  = "solid",   grid_linewidth = 0.5) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   grid_colour    = "grey80",   grid_linetype  = "dotted",   grid_linewidth = 0.4) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   grid_linetype = "blank")

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: Control lines separating texture class regions. Bold black borders:  Hidden borders — set border_colour = NA:
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "wiki",   border_colour    = "black",   border_linewidth = 1.0) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "wiki",   border_colour = NA)

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: tick_height = NULL (default) auto-scales break density. Set 0 hide tick marks keeping grid lines labels.  Set fixed height (Cartesian units) make ticks longer shorter:
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   breaks      = seq(10, 90, 10),   tick_height = 0) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   tick_height = 0.05)

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: texture_label_style() returns validated list label_style argument. Supply keys want override.

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: # Hide percentage labels, keep ticks and grid gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   breaks      = seq(10, 90, 10),   label_style = texture_label_style(tick_show = FALSE)) # Larger tick labels gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(tick_size = 3.5))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: # Bare triangle — no axis names, just arrows gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(axis_show = FALSE)) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(axis_size = 5))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "wiki",   label_style = texture_label_style(class_show = FALSE)) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(class_size = 3.5))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(     tick_show  = FALSE,     axis_show  = FALSE,     class_show = FALSE))

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: texture_surface() constructs IDW-interpolated surface scattered ternary sample points. Pass surface argument gg_texture_triangle(); fill scale left open scale_fill_*() call.  resolution controls interpolation grid density (default 150). Higher = smoother, slower.  power controls IDW locality (default 2). Higher = local, spikier.  Surface wiki-style class borders hidden class labels clean heatmap look:
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "prop", option = "plasma") gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,                              resolution = 40L)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +   ggtitle("resolution = 40")  gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,                              resolution = 250L)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +   ggtitle("resolution = 250") gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,                              power = 1)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +   ggtitle("power = 1 (smooth)")  gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,                              power = 5)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +   ggtitle("power = 5 (local)") gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface          = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop),   border_colour    = "white",   border_linewidth = 0.6,   grid_colour      = "white",   grid_linetype    = "solid",   grid_linewidth   = 0.15,   label_style      = texture_label_style(class_show = FALSE)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "prop", option = "magma")

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: geom_texture_contour() returns ggplot2 layer adds iso-lines specified breaks. works texture triangle, without surface.

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay) +   geom_texture_contour(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,     breaks    = c(10, 20, 30),     colour    = "steelblue",     linewidth = 0.8)

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay, style = "wiki") +   geom_texture_contour(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,     breaks    = 20,     colour    = "black",     linewidth = 1.4,     linetype  = "dashed")

what: Contours on a surface — threshold annotation
title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: single bold contour highlighting threshold (e.g. prop = 20):
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "prop", option = "plasma") +   geom_texture_contour(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,     breaks    = 20,     colour    = "white",     linewidth = 1.6)

title: gg_texture_triangle: Full API Reference
text: publication-ready black--white triangle 10 % grid, tick labels, bold borders, labelled sample points:  full annotated heatmap 5 % reference grid threshold contour:
code: gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   colour           = label,   breaks           = seq(10, 90, 10),   grid_colour      = "grey50",   grid_linetype    = "dotted",   border_colour    = "grey20",   border_linewidth = 0.6,   tick_height      = 0.018,   label_style      = texture_label_style(     tick_show  = FALSE,     axis_size  = 3.8,     class_size = 2.2)) +   scale_colour_grey(name = NULL, end = 0.3) gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface          = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay,                                      z = prop, resolution = 200L),   breaks           = seq(10, 90, 10),   grid_colour      = "white",   grid_linetype    = "solid",   grid_linewidth   = 0.2,   border_colour    = "white",   border_linewidth = 0.7,   label_style      = texture_label_style(     class_show = FALSE,     tick_show  = FALSE,     axis_size  = 3.5)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "prop", option = "inferno") +   geom_texture_contour(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop,     breaks = 20, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5, linetype = "dashed")

what: 1.1 Load a sand / silt / clay raster stack
title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: practice raster stack typically comes downloaded product SoilGrids ESDAC. requirement SpatRaster named layers sand, silt, clay (%). vignette build synthetic stack mimics 20 × 20 km catchment sandy upland area clay-rich valley floor:
code: library(terra)  # Load a 3-band GeoTIFF where bands are named sand, silt, clay r <- terra::rast("path/to/soil_texture_stack.tif")  # Rename layers if needed names(r) <- c("sand", "silt", "clay")  # Inspect print(r) terra::plot(r, main = c("Sand (%)", "Silt (%)", "Clay (%)")) library(terra) #> terra 1.9.11  set.seed(1847)  # 50 × 50 cell grid, 400 m resolution, EPSG:32632 (UTM zone 32N) r_template <- terra::rast(   nrows = 50, ncols = 50,   xmin = 0, xmax = 20000,   ymin = 0, ymax = 20000,   crs  = "EPSG:32632" )  # Simulate a north–south valley: clay increases toward centre column col_idx  <- terra::colFromX(r_template, terra::xFromCol(r_template,                               seq_len(terra::ncol(r_template)))) valley   <- dnorm(seq(0, 1, length.out = terra::ncol(r_template)),                   mean = 0.5, sd = 0.18) valley_m <- matrix(rep(valley, each = terra::nrow(r_template)),                    nrow = terra::nrow(r_template))  # Sand decreases toward valley; clay increases sand_m <- pmax(15, pmin(85, 70 - 45 * valley_m +                matrix(rnorm(2500, 0, 5), 50, 50))) clay_m <- pmax(5,  pmin(55, 10 + 40 * valley_m +                matrix(rnorm(2500, 0, 4), 50, 50))) silt_m <- 100 - sand_m - clay_m  # Force silt ≥ 2 (push excess into sand) excess <- pmin(0, silt_m - 2) sand_m <- sand_m + excess silt_m <- silt_m - excess  # Assemble into SpatRaster r <- c(terra::setValues(r_template, sand_m),        terra::setValues(r_template, silt_m),        terra::setValues(r_template, clay_m)) names(r) <- c("sand", "silt", "clay")  print(r) #> class       : SpatRaster  #> size        : 50, 50, 3  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 400, 400  (x, y) #> extent      : 0, 20000, 0, 20000  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 32N (EPSG:32632)  #> source(s)   : memory #> names       :     sand,   silt, clay  #> min values  : 15.00000,  2.000,    5  #> max values  : 80.93731, 43.958,   55

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
code: terra::summary(r) #>       sand            silt            clay       #>  Min.   :15.00   Min.   : 2.00   Min.   : 5.00   #>  1st Qu.:15.00   1st Qu.:20.79   1st Qu.:20.06   #>  Median :33.11   Median :28.28   Median :42.67   #>  Mean   :36.85   Mean   :25.26   Mean   :37.89   #>  3rd Qu.:58.54   3rd Qu.:30.00   3rd Qu.:55.00   #>  Max.   :80.94   Max.   :43.96   Max.   :55.00 terra::plot(r,   main      = c("Sand (%)", "Silt (%)", "Clay (%)"),   col       = grDevices::hcl.colors(50, "YlOrBr", rev = TRUE),   axes      = FALSE )

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: Pass SpatRaster directly classify_texture(). sand, silt, clay arguments name layers (default "sand", "silt", "clay" can omitted ): output single-layer categorical SpatRaster. Integer cell values mapped texture class names via raster attribute table (RAT): Count many cells fall class:
code: r_class <- classify_texture(r)   # layer names match defaults print(r_class) #> class       : SpatRaster  #> size        : 50, 50, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 400, 400  (x, y) #> extent      : 0, 20000, 0, 20000  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 32N (EPSG:32632)  #> source(s)   : memory #> categories  : texture_class  #> name        : texture_class  #> min value   :          clay  #> max value   :    loamy sand terra::levels(r_class)[[1]] #>    id   texture_class #> 1   1            clay #> 2   2      silty clay #> 3   3      sandy clay #> 4   4       clay loam #> 5   5 silty clay loam #> 6   6 sandy clay loam #> 7   7            loam #> 8   8      silty loam #> 9   9      sandy loam #> 10 10            silt #> 11 11      loamy sand #> 12 12            sand terra::freq(r_class) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(count)) #>   layer           value count #> 1     1            clay  1297 #> 2     1      sandy loam   597 #> 3     1 sandy clay loam   350 #> 4     1       clay loam   167 #> 5     1            loam    65 #> 6     1      sandy clay    21 #> 7     1      loamy sand     2 #> 8     1 silty clay loam     1

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: terra::plot() recognises categorical rasters automatically draws colour legend:  Side--side sand layer confirm spatial pattern:
code: terra::plot(r_class,   main  = "USDA Texture Classification",   axes  = FALSE,   mar   = c(2, 1, 2, 8)   # extra right margin for legend ) oldpar <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(1, 1, 2, 6))  terra::plot(r[["sand"]],   main = "Sand (%)",   col  = grDevices::hcl.colors(50, "YlOrBr", rev = TRUE),   axes = FALSE)  terra::plot(r_class,   main = "Texture class",   axes = FALSE,   mar  = c(1, 1, 2, 10)) par(oldpar)

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: output standard SpatRaster can written GDAL-supported format. GeoTIFF:
code: terra::writeRaster(r_class, "texture_classification.tif",                    datatype = "INT1U", overwrite = TRUE)

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: point observations (e.g. soil survey pedons, lab samples coordinates) sf method appends .texture_class .texture_abbr preserving geometry:
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  # 30 synthetic pedon locations across the study area set.seed(3312) pedons <- sf::st_as_sf(   tibble::tibble(     pedon_id = sprintf("P%02d", 1:30),     x        = runif(30, 500,  19500),     y        = runif(30, 500,  19500),     sand     = runif(30, 10,   80),     clay     = runif(30,  5,   45)   ) |>     dplyr::mutate(silt = 100 - sand - clay) |>     dplyr::filter(silt >= 2),   coords = c("x", "y"),   crs    = "EPSG:32632" )  # Classify — bare column names, tidy-eval, geometry preserved pedons_class <- classify_texture(pedons, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay)  dplyr::select(pedons_class, pedon_id, sand, silt, clay,               .texture_class, .texture_abbr) #> Simple feature collection with 28 features and 6 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 688.112 ymin: 564.7616 xmax: 19440.82 ymax: 19114.22 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / UTM zone 32N #> # A tibble: 28 × 7 #>    pedon_id  sand  silt  clay .texture_class  .texture_abbr #>    <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>           <chr>         #>  1 P01       13.8  53.7  32.5 silty clay loam SiClLo        #>  2 P03       34.2  35.6  30.2 clay loam       ClLo          #>  3 P04       40.0  35.8  24.2 loam            Lo            #>  4 P05       41.7  34.9  23.4 loam            Lo            #>  5 P06       43.6  28.8  27.6 clay loam       ClLo          #>  6 P07       28.3  45.0  26.7 loam            Lo            #>  7 P08       29.1  60.1  10.8 silty loam      SiLo          #>  8 P09       56.0  17.5  26.5 sandy clay loam SaClLo        #>  9 P10       41.1  15.9  42.9 clay            Cl            #> 10 P11       56.5  23.6  19.9 sandy loam      SaLo          #> # ℹ 18 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: geometry <POINT [m]>

what: 2.1 Plot points over the classified raster
title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
code: terra::plot(r_class,   main = "Texture class + pedon locations",   axes = FALSE,   mar  = c(1, 1, 2, 10) ) plot(sf::st_geometry(pedons_class),   add  = TRUE,   pch  = 21,   bg   = "white",   cex  = 0.9 )

title: GIS Workflow: Classifying Raster and Vector Soil Data
text: SoilGrids delivers sand/silt/clay separate GeoTIFFs (e.g. sand_0-5cm_mean.tif). downloading, stack rename:
code: library(terra)  r <- terra::rast(c(   "sand_0-5cm_mean.tif",   "silt_0-5cm_mean.tif",   "clay_0-5cm_mean.tif" )) names(r) <- c("sand", "silt", "clay")  # SoilGrids values are in g/kg — convert to % r <- r / 10  # Classify r_class <- classify_texture(r) terra::writeRaster(r_class, "usda_texture_class.tif",                    datatype = "INT1U", overwrite = TRUE)

what: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
text: Tidy tools soil texture classification ternary visualisation. Pipe-compatible functions accept return tibbles, tidy evaluation support sand/silt/clay column arguments. Classifies samples 12 USDA texture classes plots publication-ready texture triangles via ggplot2.

title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
code: # Install from GitHub: pak::pak("Taakefyrsten/tidysoiltexture")

title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
code: library(tidysoiltexture) library(tibble)  soils <- tribble(   ~id,   ~sand, ~silt, ~clay,   "A",      65,    20,    15,   "B",      20,    50,    30,   "C",       8,    72,    20,   "D",      40,    38,    22 )  # Classify texture classify_texture(soils, sand, silt, clay) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   id     sand  silt  clay .texture_class .texture_abbr #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr> #> 1 A        65    20    15  sandy loam     SaLo #> 2 B        20    50    30  clay loam      ClLo #> 3 C         8    72    20  silty loam     SiLo #> 4 D        40    38    22  loam           Lo  # Plot the texture triangle gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand, silt, clay, colour = id)

title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
text: classify_texture() dispatches automatically sf point objects terra SpatRaster stacks — extra code needed:
code: # sf point object library(sf) pts_sf <- st_as_sf(soils_with_coords, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326) classify_texture(pts_sf, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay)  # terra SpatRaster (e.g. from SoilGrids — divide by 10 to convert g/kg to %) library(terra) r <- rast("soilgrids_sand_silt_clay.tif") r_class <- classify_texture(r / 10, sand = "sand", silt = "silt", clay = "clay")

title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
text: vectorised backend iterates 12 USDA polygon classes, testing N points per class simultaneously — row-level R loops.

title: tidysoiltexture — Soil texture classification and visualisation
text: vignette("classify-texture") — dispatch methods, edge cases, performance vignette("gg-texture-triangle") — full styling reference vignette("gis-workflow") — sf terra integration Full documentation: https://taakefyrsten.github.io/tidysoiltexture

what: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
title: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
text: Classifies row data USDA soil texture class. Dispatches class data, function works plain data frames tibbles, sf point objects, terra SpatRaster stacks.

title: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
code: classify_texture(data, sand, silt, clay, system = "USDA", ...)  # Default S3 method classify_texture(data, sand, silt, clay, system = "USDA", ...)  # S3 method for class 'sf' classify_texture(data, sand, silt, clay, system = "USDA", ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' classify_texture(   data,   sand = "sand",   silt = "silt",   clay = "clay",   system = "USDA",   ... )

title: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
text: data data frame / tibble, sf object, SpatRaster. sand data frames sf: bare column name (scalar) giving sand percentage (0–100). SpatRaster: character layer name (default "sand"). silt sand, silt percentage. clay sand, clay percentage. system Classification system. Currently "USDA" supported. ... Reserved future use.

title: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
text: data frame / tibble: input two appended columns, .texture_class .texture_abbr. sf: input sf object two columns appended, geometry preserved. SpatRaster: single-layer categorical SpatRaster named "texture_class", integer cell values mapped class names via terra::levels().

title: Classify soil texture from sand, silt, and clay percentages — classify_texture
code: # --- data frame / tibble ------------------------------------------------- soils <- tibble::tibble(   sand = c(70, 20, 40, 10),   silt = c(15, 30, 40, 20),   clay = c(15, 50, 20, 70) ) classify_texture(soils, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay) #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>    sand  silt  clay .texture_class .texture_abbr #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>          <chr>         #> 1    70    15    15 sandy loam     SaLo          #> 2    20    30    50 clay           Cl            #> 3    40    40    20 loam           Lo            #> 4    10    20    70 clay           Cl             # --- sf point object ----------------------------------------------------- if (requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {   pts <- data.frame(     sand = c(70, 20, 40, 10),     silt = c(15, 30, 40, 20),     clay = c(15, 50, 20, 70),     lon  = c(10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 10.4),     lat  = c(59.1, 59.2, 59.3, 59.4)   )   pts_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(pts, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)   classify_texture(pts_sf, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay) } #> Simple feature collection with 4 features and 5 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 10.1 ymin: 59.1 xmax: 10.4 ymax: 59.4 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>   sand silt clay          geometry .texture_class .texture_abbr #> 1   70   15   15 POINT (10.1 59.1)     sandy loam          SaLo #> 2   20   30   50 POINT (10.2 59.2)           clay            Cl #> 3   40   40   20 POINT (10.3 59.3)           loam            Lo #> 4   10   20   70 POINT (10.4 59.4)           clay            Cl  # --- terra SpatRaster stack ---------------------------------------------- if (requireNamespace("terra", quietly = TRUE)) {   r <- terra::rast(ncols = 10, nrows = 10, nlyrs = 3)   names(r) <- c("sand", "silt", "clay")   terra::values(r) <- c(     runif(100, 10, 80),   # sand     runif(100, 5,  50),   # silt     runif(100, 5,  40)    # clay — will have invalid sums; demo only   )   classify_texture(r, sand = "sand", silt = "silt", clay = "clay") } #> Error in check_texture_sums(sand_v, silt_v, clay_v): Sand, silt, and clay must sum to 100 for every row. #> ✖ Found 96 row(s) where the sum differs from 100 by more than 1.

what: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
text: Interpolates numeric variable scattered ternary sample points onto regular Cartesian grid (using IDW) draws contour lines specified values. Add plot produced gg_texture_triangle() +.

title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
code: geom_texture_contour(   data,   sand,   silt,   clay,   z,   breaks = NULL,   resolution = 150L,   power = 2,   ... )

title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
text: data data frame containing sample points. sand, silt, clay Bare column names ternary coordinates (0–100). z Bare column name numeric value interpolate. breaks Numeric vector z values draw contour lines. Passed ggplot2::geom_contour(). NULL (default), ggplot2 chooses breaks automatically. resolution Integer. Interpolation grid width (default 150L). power Positive numeric. IDW exponent (default 2). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_contour() (e.g. linewidth, colour, linetype).

title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
text: geom_contour ggplot2 layer.

title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
text: Contour lines rendered top triangle layers, making suitable highlighting analytical thresholds (e.g. significance boundary p = 0.05).

title: Add iso-contour lines to a texture triangle plot — geom_texture_contour
code: set.seed(7) surf_df <- tibble::tibble(   sand = runif(80, 5, 90),   clay = runif(80, 5, 60),   silt = 100 - sand - clay ) |>   dplyr::filter(silt >= 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(p_val = pnorm(scale(clay)[, 1]))  pts <- tibble::tibble(sand = c(40, 20), silt = c(40, 30), clay = c(20, 50))  gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay) +   geom_texture_contour(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = p_val,                        breaks = 0.05, linewidth = 1.4, colour = "red") #> Warning: `stat_contour()`: Zero contours were generated #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf

what: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
title: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
text: Produces ggplot2 object displaying soil texture triangle texture class region drawn filled polygon optional sample points overlaid. Triangle geometry built usda_texture_classes using internal ternary--Cartesian coordinate conversion.

title: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
code: gg_texture_triangle(   data,   sand,   silt,   clay,   colour = NULL,   system = "USDA",   style = c("none", "wiki"),   breaks = c(20, 40, 60, 80),   grid_colour = "grey70",   grid_linetype = "dashed",   grid_linewidth = 0.25,   border_colour = "grey40",   border_linewidth = 0.35,   tick_height = NULL,   surface = NULL,   label_style = list() )

title: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
text: data data frame tibble containing sample points plot. sand Bare column name (scalar) sand percentage (0–100). silt Bare column name (scalar) silt percentage (0–100). clay Bare column name (scalar) clay percentage (0–100). colour Optional bare column name map point colour. Pass NULL (default) colour mapping. system Classification system; currently "USDA" supported. style Fill colour style texture class polygons. "none" (default) draws unfilled polygons; "wiki" applies colour scheme used Wikipedia USDA soil texture diagram. breaks Numeric vector percentage values draw grid lines tick marks (values 1 99). Defaults c(20, 40, 60, 80). Common alternatives: c(25, 50, 75) (quarters), seq(10, 90, 10) (deciles), seq(5, 95, 5) (5 % steps). grid_colour Colour internal grid lines. Defaults "grey70". grid_linetype Line type internal grid lines. Defaults "dashed". value accepted ggplot2::geom_line() valid (e.g. "solid", "dotted", "blank" hide grid entirely). grid_linewidth Stroke width internal grid lines. Defaults 0.25. border_colour Colour texture class boundary lines. Defaults "grey40". Set NA hide class borders. border_linewidth Stroke width class boundary lines. Defaults 0.35. tick_height Height tick marks drawn triangle edge, Cartesian coordinate units. Defaults NULL, auto-scales break density. Set 0 hide tick marks keeping grid lines labels. surface Optional. "texture_surface" object produced texture_surface(), specifying numeric variable interpolate display continuous fill gradient across triangle. supplied, style palette fill suppressed favour surface; fill scale left unset user can control freely + ggplot2::scale_fill_*(). iso-contour lines top surface (plain triangle), see geom_texture_contour(). label_style Named list text styling overrides. Prefer building texture_label_style() argument completion validation. Recognised keys: tick_show Show tick-mark percentage labels (default TRUE). tick_size Font size tick labels. Defaults auto-scale break density (2.2 / 1.8 / 1.4 ≤5 / ≤10 / >10 breaks). axis_show Show axis name labels (default TRUE). axis_size Font size axis name labels (default 3.2). class_show Show texture class name labels inside polygons (default TRUE). class_size Font size texture class labels (default 2.5). keys supply overridden; unspecified keys use defaults.

title: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a soil texture triangle with sample points — gg_texture_triangle
code: soils <- tibble::tibble(   sand = c(70, 20, 40, 10),   silt = c(15, 30, 40, 20),   clay = c(15, 50, 20, 70) )  # Defaults gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand = sand, silt = silt, clay = clay)   # Wiki colours, 10 % grid, no class labels gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand, silt, clay,   style = "wiki", breaks = seq(10, 90, 10),   label_style = list(class_show = FALSE))   # Solid grid in dark grey, hide tick labels gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand, silt, clay,   grid_colour = "grey40", grid_linetype = "solid",   label_style = list(tick_show = FALSE))

what: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
title: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
text: Constructs validated list label styling overrides label_style argument gg_texture_triangle(). specify arguments want change; others inherited function defaults.

title: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
code: texture_label_style(   tick_show = NULL,   tick_size = NULL,   axis_show = NULL,   axis_size = NULL,   class_show = NULL,   class_size = NULL )

title: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
text: tick_show Logical. Show tick-mark percentage labels alongside edge tick. Default (omitted): TRUE. tick_size Positive numeric. Font size tick labels. Default: auto- scaled break density (2.2 / 1.8 / 1.4 ≤5 / ≤10 / >10 breaks). axis_show Logical. Show axis name labels ("Clay (%)" etc.) along triangle side. Default: TRUE. axis_size Positive numeric. Font size axis name labels. Default: 3.2. class_show Logical. Show texture class names inside polygon region. Default: TRUE. class_size Positive numeric. Font size texture class labels. Default: 2.5.

title: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
text: named list suitable passing label_style argument gg_texture_triangle(). keys explicitly supplied included, function defaults remain active anything left unspecified.

title: Styling options for text elements in the texture triangle — texture_label_style
code: soils <- tibble::tibble(   sand = c(70, 20, 40),   silt = c(15, 30, 40),   clay = c(15, 50, 20) )  # Hide class names, increase axis label size gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(class_show = FALSE, axis_size = 4.5))   # Bare triangle — only axis arrows remain gg_texture_triangle(soils, sand, silt, clay,   label_style = texture_label_style(tick_show = FALSE,                                     axis_show = FALSE,                                     class_show = FALSE))

what: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
text: Constructs validates surface specification use surface argument gg_texture_triangle(). Resolves sand/silt/clay/z columns data frame stores IDW interpolation settings. actual grid computation deferred plotting.

title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
code: texture_surface(data, sand, silt, clay, z, resolution = 150L, power = 2)

title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
text: data data frame containing sample points ternary coordinates numeric response variable. sand, silt, clay Bare column names ternary coordinates (0–100). z Bare column name numeric value interpolate display fill colour. resolution Integer. Number grid cells along x-axis interpolation grid. Higher values give smoother surfaces cost speed. Default 150L. power Positive numeric. IDW distance-decay exponent. Higher values give data point local influence. Default 2.

title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
text: object class "texture_surface" use gg_texture_triangle().

title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
text: Interpolation performed using Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW) Cartesian space; external packages required.

title: Prepare a surface for interpolated rendering on a texture triangle — texture_surface
code: # Generate synthetic data with a property that varies across texture space set.seed(42) surf_df <- tibble::tibble(   sand = runif(60, 5, 90),   clay = runif(60, 5, 60),   silt = 100 - sand - clay ) |>   dplyr::filter(silt >= 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(prop = sand * 0.3 + clay * 0.5 + rnorm(dplyr::n(), 0, 5))  pts <- tibble::tibble(sand = c(40, 20), silt = c(40, 30), clay = c(20, 50))  gg_texture_triangle(pts, sand, silt, clay,   surface = texture_surface(surf_df, sand, silt, clay, z = prop)) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "prop")

what: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
title: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
text: tidy data frame containing polygon vertex coordinates 12 USDA soil texture classes, suitable drawing texture triangle gg_texture_triangle() point--polygon classification classify_texture().

title: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
code: usda_texture_classes

title: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
text: tibble 58 rows 6 columns: class Full texture class name (e.g. "clay loam"). abbr Short abbreviation (e.g. "ClLo"). vertex_order Integer giving order vertex within polygon. sand Sand percentage vertex. silt Silt percentage vertex. clay Clay percentage vertex.

title: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
text: Extracted soiltexture package (TT.classes.tbl() / TT.vertices.tbl(), system "USDA.TT"). See data-raw/usda_texture_classes.R preparation script.

title: USDA soil texture class polygon vertices — usda_texture_classes
text: Polygon vertices listed order around polygon. Sand, silt, clay values percent (0–100) sum 100 per vertex.

title: tidysoiltexture 1.0.0
text: Initial release.

title: tidysoiltexture 1.0.0
text: classify_texture() — USDA texture classification data frames, sf point objects, terra SpatRaster stacks via S3 dispatch. Fully vectorised implementation; classifies 10 000 samples < 0.015 s. gg_texture_triangle() — ggplot2-based texture triangle customisable grid lines, borders, axis ticks, class labels, fill surfaces, contours. texture_surface() — constructs IDW-interpolated continuous surface object point data use gg_texture_triangle(). geom_texture_contour() — adds iso-contour lines texture triangle plot. texture_label_style() — validates constructs label style lists controlling tick, axis, class label appearance.

title: tidysoiltexture 1.0.0
text: usda_texture_classes — polygon boundary data 12 USDA texture classes ternary (sand, silt, clay) coordinates.

title: tidysoiltexture 1.0.0
text: Spatial methods require sf (≥ 1.0) terra (≥ 1.6), listed Suggests. clear error raised via rlang::check_installed() relevant package absent. SoilGrids / ESDAC rasters store fractions g/kg (0–1000); cli::cli_warn() fires automatically values exceed 100.
